module_description: Teach your program to think — make choices based on conditions.

concepts.title: if statements
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid can write an if statement that runs code only when a condition is True

analogies.text: An if statement is like a bouncer at a door. If you meet the condition (you're tall enough), you get in. If not, nothing happens.
Think of if like a fork in the road. Python checks the condition — if it's True, it takes that path. If False, it skips it.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: age is 12, which is greater than 10 — so the condition is True and the indented code runs.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: score is 5, which is not greater than 10 — condition is False, so the indented code is skipped.

questions.question: This code has a syntax error. Find it.
options: Missing colon : at the end of the if line
print needs quotes around Big number
questions.answer: Missing colon : at the end of the if line
questions.explanation: Every if statement needs a colon : at the end. if x > 15: — the colon tells Python the condition is done.

questions.question: Fill in the operator to check if the password matches.
questions.explanation: == checks if two values are equal. We want to check if the entered password matches "python123".

questions.question: Write code that asks for a number and prints 'Positive!' if the number is greater than 0.
questions.explanation: Get the number with int(input()), then use if num > 0: to check.

concepts.title: if / else
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid can write if/else and knows exactly one branch always runs

analogies.text: if/else is like a coin flip decision. If heads — do this. Else (tails) — do that. One of the two always happens. Never both, never neither.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: 30 is greater than 25, so the if condition is True. The if branch runs. The else is skipped.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: 4 % 2 = 0, and 0 == 0 is True — so 4 is even. The if branch prints Even.

questions.question: Write code that asks for a password. If it's 'secret', print 'Welcome!'. Otherwise print 'Wrong password!'
questions.check: contains if and else and print(
questions.explanation: Use if pw == "secret": for the match, else: for everything else.

concepts.title: if / elif / else
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid can write multi-branch logic with elif and predict which branch runs

analogies.text: elif is like a multiple choice question. If A matches — pick A. Elif B matches — pick B. Elif C — pick C. Else — none of the above. Only one answer runs.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: 75 is not >= 90, not >= 80, but IS >= 70. So the third branch runs and prints C.

questions.question: What is wrong with this code?
options: else cannot have a condition — use elif
questions.answer: else cannot have a condition — use elif
questions.explanation: else never has a condition. If you want to check another condition, use elif.

questions.question: Ask the user their age. Print 'Child' if under 13, 'Teen' if 13-17, 'Adult' if 18 or older.
questions.check: contains elif and if and else
questions.explanation: Check youngest first: if < 13, elif < 18, else for 18+.

concepts.title: Logical operators — and, or, not
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid can use and/or/not in conditions and predict the boolean result

analogies.text: 'and' is strict — both things must be true. 'or' is flexible — at least one must be true. 'not' flips it — True becomes False and False becomes True.

questions.code:
age = 15
has_ticket = True
if age >= 13 and has_ticket:
    print("Enjoy the movie!")
questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: age >= 13 is True AND has_ticket is True. Both are True so the and condition is True.

questions.code:
raining = False
cold = True
if raining or cold:
    print("Bring a jacket!")
questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: raining is False but cold is True. With or, just one needs to be True. So the condition is True.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: not flips the boolean. not False becomes True.

concepts.title: Nested if statements
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid can write an if inside an if and trace which code runs

analogies.text: Nested ifs are like locked rooms inside locked rooms. First you unlock the outer door (outer if). Only then can you try the inner door (inner if).

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: age is 20 so outer if passes. has_id is True so inner if passes. Prints Entry allowed.

concepts.title: Indentation rules
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid understands that indentation controls which code belongs to which block

analogies.text: Indentation in Python is like bullet points in an outline. The main point is at the left. Sub-points are indented — they belong to the point above them.

questions.code:
x = 5
if x > 3:
    print("Inside if")
print("Always prints")
questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: The indented print is inside the if. The unindented print is outside — it always runs regardless.

questions.question: What is wrong?
questions.explanation: Code inside an if must be indented with 4 spaces (or one tab). Without indentation Python doesn't know what belongs to the if.

concepts.title: Truthy and Falsy values
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid knows that 0, empty string, and None are falsy in Python conditions

analogies.text: In Python, some values act like False without being False. Zero, empty strings, and None are 'falsy' — they fail an if check. Everything else is 'truthy'.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: An empty string "" is falsy in Python. The if condition fails, so else runs.

questions.question: What does this print?
questions.explanation: 0 is falsy in Python. Even though score has a value, 0 acts like False in an if statement.

concepts.title: Mini Project 3 — Quiz Game
concepts.mastery_definition: Kid builds a 3-question quiz with score tracking using if/elif/else

mini_project.title: Quiz Game
mini_project.description: Build a 3-question quiz. Track the score and give a grade at the end.
mini_project.success_criteria: 3 questions, correct answer checking, score total, grade message at end
